Container.Name: Fodaris
Container.Overview:
Fodaris - self-hosted infrastructure monitoring platform. Monitor servers, containers, network devices, websites, databases, and more from a single dashboard.
Includes a built-in PostgreSQL database, TLS (auto-generated self-signed cert), and a React-based web UI accessible on port 8443.
After installation, open https://your-unraid-ip:8443 and log in with admin / Fodaris2026. You will be prompted to change your password on first login.
Container.Description:
Fodaris is a self-hosted monitoring platform for homelabs and small businesses. Monitor Linux, Windows, and macOS endpoints, Docker containers, network devices (SNMP), websites, and databases from one place.
Data stays on your hardware. No cloud accounts, no subscriptions, no telemetry.

Config.Name: Web UI Port (HTTPS)
Config.Description: Port for the Fodaris web interface. Access via https://your-unraid-ip:8443

Config.Name: Encryption Key
Config.Description: Secret key for encrypting stored data. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Save this value — if lost, stored data cannot be decrypted. Leave blank to auto-generate (key lost on container restart).

Config.Name: Server Name
Config.Description: Display name for this Fodaris server instance.

Config.Name: Metric Retention (days)
Config.Description: How many days of metric history to retain. Higher values use more disk space.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the Fodaris database and metrics.

Config.Name: TLS Certificates (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: path to a directory containing fodaris.crt and fodaris.key for custom TLS. Leave blank to use the auto-generated self-signed certificate.

Config.Name: Host /proc
Config.Description: Mounts the host process filesystem so Fodaris can monitor host processes and sensors.

Config.Name: Host /sys
Config.Description: Mounts the host sysfs so Fodaris can read hardware sensor data.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Grants Fodaris read-only access to Docker for container monitoring.
